D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Perkin's Mauveine — The First Synthetic Dye (1856)

18-year-old William Henry Perkin tried to synthesise quinine from coal-tar aniline; he produced a purple sludge instead and founded the synthetic-dye industry

Perkin oxidised aniline with potassium dichromate. The black tar washed out a mauve dye that stained silk permanently. Within 10 years coal-tar dyes (mauveine, fuchsine, magenta) had collapsed the natural-dye economy and built BASF, Bayer, and Hoechst — the foundation of the modern chemical industry.

Scrub timeline 1856 → 1900 to see colour palette expand from one purple to a rainbow.
